cross_scene_arcs.title: The Protector's Resolve
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Dewey consistently prioritizes Sidney's safety, evolving from physical intervention to strategic protection planning, despite her resistance and the systemic dismissal of his expertise.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Dewey physically rescues Sidney from a hostile press swarm, establishing his role as her protector.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Dewey coordinates police protection for Sidney despite her emotional withdrawal, showing persistence in his protective mission.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Sidney's belief that no place is safe undermines Dewey's efforts, and the killer remains at large, threatening the success of his goal.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Reclaiming Authority and Dignity
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Dewey transitions from being marginalized by authorities to reasserting his investigative value, culminating in targeted suspicion of the killers based on forensic insight.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Dewey is dismissed by law enforcement despite offering crucial context about the killer's pattern.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Dewey identifies Joel's medical knowledge as a key clue, shifting the investigation toward the real perpetrators.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Despite his contributions, Dewey remains outside the official chain of command, limiting his ability to act decisively.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Fractured Bond with Gale
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Dewey and Gale navigate a complex emotional history, moving from confrontation to fragile reconciliation, only for their connection to be tragically severed by violence.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Dewey confronts Gale over her book, rejecting her apology and asserting his self-worth.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Dewey dies trying to save Gale, his final act underscoring enduring care despite unresolved emotional tension.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Gale is left unable to process Dewey's emotional revelation before his death, leaving their relationship suspended in grief and ambiguity.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Confronting the Unseen Threat
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Dewey engages in an escalating confrontation with the killer, progressing from forensic analysis to direct pursuit, ultimately becoming a victim of the killer's omniscient design.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Dewey investigates the crime scene anomaly—the missing body despite heavy bloodshed—sensing a calculated killer.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Dewey discovers the killer is live-streaming and is murdered while attempting to rescue Gale, becoming part of the killer's spectacle.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The killer's full plan remains hidden until Dewey's death, emphasizing the futility of traditional investigative methods against a media-savvy predator.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Marginalized Figure to Tragic Hero
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Dewey evolves from a sidelined figure grappling with past trauma to an emotionally mature and active participant in the investigation, only to meet a symbolic and tragic end that underscores the film's themes of vulnerability and spectacle.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Dewey is emotionally withdrawn and professionally dismissed, struggling to be heard despite his experience.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Dewey is found impaled on a set wall, his death staged as a macabre spectacle, marking the collapse of institutional safety and his personal journey.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: His survival remains ambiguous, leaving open whether his death is real or part of the killers' psychological warfare.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Exploitation of Tragedy: From Author to Accomplice
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Gale's role as the author of a book that sensationalized real trauma evolves into active complicity in exploiting personal suffering for media gain. Her narrative authority, initially celebrated, becomes ethically compromised as she manipulates events—orchestrating confrontations and prioritizing spectacle over truth—leading to a crisis of conscience when confronted with the human cost of her work.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Gale's book is publicly celebrated as the basis of a horror film, cementing her influence and notoriety.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Gale is emotionally destabilized after Cotton confronts her about exploiting his suffering, forcing her to question whether her journalism served justice or perpetuated harm.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The moral consequences of Gale's storytelling remain unaddressed—whether she will be held accountable or continue to shape narratives at the expense of victims.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Reclaiming Relevance: Gale's Struggle for Professional and Personal Validation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Gale fights to reestablish herself as a credible journalist and investigative force despite claims of retirement and public skepticism. Through strategic insight and assertive presence, she gains official recognition from law enforcement and steers the investigation, yet her status remains precarious due to personal rivalries and emotional vulnerabilities.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Gale returns to field reporting under public scrutiny and professional challenge from rival journalist Debbie Salt.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Gale is recognized by authorities as a key consultant, successfully influencing the direction of the investigation and securing Dewey as her protector.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Her credibility is still contested by peers and victims alike, and her reliance on media optics over empathy undermines her legitimacy.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Observer to Target: Gale's Descent into Danger
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: After declaring disinterest in the story, Gale is personally targeted by the killer, transforming her from a detached observer into an active participant in the horror. Her investigative instincts reactivate under threat, leading to a desperate flight for survival that strips away her professional armor and exposes her vulnerability.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Gale claims to be disengaged from the investigation, attempting to step away from the media frenzy.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Gale is hunted through campus, witnesses Dewey's murder, and shifts her survival focus toward reuniting with Sidney, abandoning institutional help.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Gale remains in immediate physical danger with no safe exit or confirmed ally, and the killer's ability to track her suggests deeper surveillance and manipulation.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Fractured Alliance: Gale and Dewey's Tense Reconciliation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Gale and Dewey's strained relationship—damaged by her exploitative portrayal of him—undergoes a fragile reconciliation as they are thrust back into partnership. Though Gale asserts control by choosing Dewey as her protector, their bond is tested by danger and culminates in shared trauma when he is murdered in front of her.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Dewey publicly rebukes Gale for exploiting him in her book, damaging their professional and personal rapport.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Gale witnesses Dewey's murder, marking a devastating emotional turning point that shatters any remaining emotional distance between them.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Gale is left to process Dewey's death without closure, and the full impact of his loss on her transformation remains unexplored.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Moral Awakening and Final Reckoning
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Gale experiences a shift from tabloid ambition to moral introspection, briefly choosing empathy over exploitation. Yet her attempt to transcend her past is undercut by continued exclusion and ultimately destroyed when she is silenced—both physically and narratively—by those who weaponize the very media dynamics she once controlled.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Gale abandons a potential interview with Sidney out of compassion, signaling a break from her sensationalist persona.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Gale is murdered while attempting to expose the truth, her final words dismissed as narrative noise, and the incriminating evidence stolen to manipulate the public story.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Gale's death raises unresolved questions about whether truth can prevail in a media landscape built on spectacle, and whether her final act of defiance will ever be recognized.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Reclamation of Agency Amid Repeating Trauma
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Sidney transitions from passive victimhood to active resistance as the new killings force her to confront the recurrence of her past trauma. Initially overwhelmed and thrust back into public scrutiny, she gradually reclaims control by making deliberate choices to face danger, reject overprotection, and isolate herself to protect others—culminating in a defiant stance against the killers.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Sidney's goal shifts from living a private college life to seeking clarity and safety as she realizes the killings have resumed and she is a target
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She successfully distances herself emotionally and physically from her friends, reinforcing her resolve to endure the trauma without relying on others
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Sidney feels guilty and trapped, torn between appreciating her friends' loyalty and resenting the loss of freedom and privacy, highlighting her growing emotional isolation

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Fractured Path to Emotional Intimacy
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Sidney's struggle to balance emotional connection with self-protection unfolds through her evolving relationship with Derek and tentative reconciliation with Randy. She alternates between vulnerability and withdrawal, revealing deep ambivalence about trust and intimacy in the aftermath of trauma.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Sidney aims to avoid public attention and emotional coddling, seeking normalcy and control in her life
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Derek shifts from active pursuit to respectful distance, redefining their relationship dynamic and placing the initiative for reconnection in Sidney's hands
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Sidney's deep-seated self-doubt and fear of relational vulnerability remain unresolved, continuing to hinder her capacity for intimacy

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Collapse of Safety and Institutional Trust
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Sidney's belief in external protection—whether from friends, police, or secure spaces—is systematically dismantled as the killer breaches every layer of defense. From media intrusion to digital stalking and the failure of armed escorts, each node reveals the futility of relying on systems meant to keep her safe.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Sidney is psychologically shifted from a state of relative normalcy to one of heightened awareness and dread, positioning her as the central figure in the unfolding horror narrative
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Now in the direct custody of the killer, Sidney's survival depends on escaping a moving vehicle with no immediate help
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Sidney remains trapped in the backseat of the car, unable to open the doors or break the windows, with no indication of how or if she will escape

cross_scene_arcs.title: Confronting the Ghosts of the Past
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Sidney is repeatedly forced to reckon with the unresolved legacy of her mother's murder and her role in the original Woodsboro tragedy. Through cinematic triggers, confrontations with Cotton, and the revelation of Mrs. Loomis's vendetta, she faces the emotional and narrative echoes of her past, revealing how deeply her identity is entangled with the violence that defined her.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: The investigation confirms the copycat nature of the killings, escalating the threat to Sidney as the next potential target in the sequence
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Sidney survives the immediate execution by Mrs. Loomis but is now hunted by Cotton Weary, who reveals himself as the final threat, leaving her in a state of desperate flight with no allies
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Sidney's safety remains in extreme jeopardy as Cotton, now fully revealed as the manipulative survivor-turned-killer, pursues her with the intent to frame her death as part of his victim narrative

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Survivor to Sacrificial Final Girl
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Sidney's status evolves from protected survivor to isolated target and ultimately to tragic martyr. Each confrontation strips away her allies and illusions of safety, culminating in a final battle where her survival becomes symbolic—only to end in mutual destruction, reframing her arc as one of inevitable sacrifice rather than triumph.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Sidney transitions from isolated terror to temporary safety as Dewey becomes a stabilizing, protective presence in the aftermath of extreme trauma
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Sidney is mortally wounded and collapses, failing to survive the confrontation
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The narrative is left incomplete, with no resolution beyond the mutual destruction of Sidney and Cotton
